subject: Mathematics
title: Hypatia of Alexandria
description: A quick win: understand Hypatia of Alexandria.

hook.text: In 400 AD, the greatest mathematician in Alexandria was a woman. The city didn't know what to do.

buildup.text: Hypatia taught philosophy and math, edited Ptolemy's astronomical tables, and advised the governor.

discovery.text: She improved astrolabes and hydrometers—scientific instruments that lasted centuries.

twist.text: A Christian mob murdered her in 415 AD, making her a symbol of knowledge destroyed by fanaticism.

climax.text: Her death marked the decline of ancient Alexandria as a center of learning.

punchline.text: The first known woman mathematician died for her ideas.

quiz.question: What was Hypatia known for?
options: Teaching mathematics and improving scientific instruments
